metadata.title: SOLID + Repository Pattern Refactoring Spec
metadata.branch_strategy: Create a new branch from feature/week11 e.g. refactor/solid-repository
metadata.description: Step-by-step plan to bring the HockeyRinkAPI into compliance with SOLID principles and introduce the Repository pattern for testable data access.

SRP: AdminController has 15 endpoints across 6 unrelated domains (users, sessions, registrations, leagues, dashboard, draft)
Token validation logic (ValidateTokenAsync / GetUserIdFromTokenAsync) is copy-pasted into AdminController, UsersController, SessionsController, TeamsController, and AuthController
IsAdminAsync() is duplicated in both AdminController and TeamsController
Session auto-activate/deactivate business logic lives inside AdminController.GetAllSessions() and SessionsController.GetSessions() (two places)
Request/response model classes (CreateSessionModel, UpdateSessionModel, ManualRegistrationModel, etc.) are defined at the bottom of AdminController.cs instead of in the Models folder
OCP: Session activation rules are hardcoded inside controller actions — adding a new rule requires modifying the controller
LSP: MockStripeService does not implement IPaymentService — it cannot be substituted through the abstraction
MockStripeService defines its own PaymentResult type that is incompatible with PaymentResponse from IPaymentService
ISP: No violations — IPaymentService is already small and focused
DIP: All controllers inject AppDbContext directly — no repository abstraction layer
SessionsController takes a concrete MockStripeService dependency in its constructor
AdminController and TeamsController implement their own IsAdminAsync() instead of using an injected abstraction or ASP.NET Core policy-based authorization

phases.name: Extract ITokenService
phases.goal: Eliminate token validation code duplicated across 5 controllers by centralising it behind a single injectable interface.
phases.risk: Low — pure extraction, no logic change

steps.description: Define the ITokenService interface

Move the GenerateToken() method from AuthController and the ValidateTokenAsync / GetUserIdFromTokenAsync logic (identical across all controllers) into TokenService : ITokenService. The token format is base64(userId|email|expiry:O).
content_outline: ValidateTokenAsync: decodes base64, checks expiry, looks up user by id, validates email match
GetUserIdFromTokenAsync: same decode + expiry + user lookup, returns userId or null

steps.description: Register TokenService: builder.Services.AddScoped<ITokenService, TokenService>();
Inject ITokenService. Replace private GenerateToken() and private ValidateTokenAsync() with calls to _tokenService. Remove the private helper methods.
Inject ITokenService. Replace all calls to private ValidateTokenAsync() and GetUserIdFromTokenAsync() with _tokenService calls. Remove private helper methods.

Inject ITokenService. Replace private ValidateTokenAsync() and GetUserIdFromTokenAsync() with _tokenService calls. Remove private helper methods. Remove concrete MockStripeService from constructor (see Phase 3).
Inject ITokenService. Replace private GetUserIdFromTokenAsync() inside IsAdminAsync() with _tokenService.GetUserIdFromTokenAsync(). Remove private helper method.
Inject ITokenService. Replace private GetUserIdFromTokenAsync() and IsAdminAsync() with _tokenService call. Remove private helper methods.

phases.name: Extract ISessionActivationService
phases.goal: Remove the ~60-line session auto-activate/deactivate foreach loop that is duplicated inside AdminController.GetAllSessions() and SessionsController.GetSessions().
phases.risk: Low — logic is identical between both controllers, just extracted

steps.description: Define the interface
content_outline: returns true if any sessions were modified (so callers know to SaveChanges)

steps.description: Move the foreach logic from AdminController verbatim. Inject AppDbContext to call SaveChangesAsync internally, or return a bool and let the caller save — prefer the latter to keep the service free of save-side-effects.
content_outline: auto-activate if RegistrationOpenDate <= now and not manually deactivated after open
auto-deactivate if RegistrationCloseDate < now OR EndDate < now and not manually modified after critical date

steps.description: Register: builder.Services.AddScoped<ISessionActivationService, SessionActivationService>();

Inject ISessionActivationService. In GetAllSessions(), replace the foreach block with: var changed = await _sessionActivationService.ApplyActivationRulesAsync(sessions); if (changed) await _dbContext.SaveChangesAsync();
Inject ISessionActivationService. In GetSessions(), replace the foreach block with the same two-line call pattern used in step 2.4.

phases.name: Fix MockStripeService LSP/DIP Violation
phases.goal: Make MockStripeService substitutable through IPaymentService so SessionsController can drop its concrete dependency.
phases.risk: Low — SessionsController already has IPaymentService; this just removes the redundant concrete one

steps.description: Option A (preferred): Delete MockStripeService entirely. MockPaymentService already fulfils the same role and correctly implements IPaymentService. Option B: Make MockStripeService implement IPaymentService by renaming PaymentResult to PaymentResponse and aligning method signature to Task<PaymentResponse> ProcessPaymentAsync(PaymentRequest). Choose Option A unless there is a reason to keep both.

Remove private readonly MockStripeService _stripeService field and the constructor parameter. Remove the throw guard for it. The controller already has IPaymentService _paymentService — that is sufficient.
Remove builder.Services.AddTransient<MockStripeService>(); — it is no longer needed.

phases.name: Move Request Models to Models/Requests
phases.goal: Remove the 7 model classes defined at the bottom of AdminController.cs and place each in its own file under Models/Requests/.
phases.risk: Low — rename only, using the same class names to avoid breaking consumers

steps.description: Move CreateSessionModel class. Keep the class name as-is OR rename to CreateSessionRequest and update the one usage in AdminController.CreateSession(). Add namespace HockeyRinkAPI.Models.Requests.
Move UpdateSessionModel. Same naming convention decision as 4.1.
Move UpdateLeagueModel.
Move ManualRegistrationModel.
Move UpdatePlayerRatingModel.
Move UpdateUserProfileModel.
Move PublishDraftModel.
Delete the 7 model class definitions at the bottom of AdminController.cs. Add using HockeyRinkAPI.Models.Requests; to the top.

phases.name: Introduce Repository Pattern
phases.goal: Abstract all direct AppDbContext calls behind repository interfaces so controllers depend on abstractions, enabling true unit testing without InMemoryDatabase.
phases.risk: Medium — touches all controllers; do one repository at a time, build after each
notes: UserManager<ApplicationUser> already serves as an adequate user repository — do NOT wrap it in a custom IUserRepository, that would add complexity with no benefit.
Keep AppDbContext registered in DI — repositories take it as a constructor dependency.
Use AddScoped for all repositories (matches DbContext lifetime).

steps.description: Define session data access interface
Implement ISessionRepository wrapping AppDbContext. Move Include() chains and Where() filters from AdminController and SessionsController here.
Define registration data access interface
Implement IRegistrationRepository.
Define league data access interface
Implement ILeagueRepository.
Define payment data access interface
Implement IPaymentRepository.

Register all repositories with Scoped lifetime: builder.Services.AddScoped<ISessionRepository, SessionRepository>(); builder.Services.AddScoped<IRegistrationRepository, RegistrationRepository>(); builder.Services.AddScoped<ILeagueRepository, LeagueRepository>(); builder.Services.AddScoped<IPaymentRepository, PaymentRepository>();

Replace direct _dbContext calls with injected ISessionRepository, IRegistrationRepository, ILeagueRepository, IPaymentRepository. Remove AppDbContext from constructor (or keep for any edge cases not covered by repositories). Build and test after this step.
Replace direct _dbContext calls with ISessionRepository, IRegistrationRepository, IPaymentRepository.
Replace direct _dbContext calls with IRegistrationRepository.
Replace direct _dbContext calls with ISessionRepository and any new team-specific repository if warranted.
Replace _db.Leagues.ToListAsync() with ILeagueRepository.GetAllOrderedByNameAsync().

phases.name: Split AdminController into domain controllers
phases.goal: Apply SRP by splitting AdminController's 15 endpoints across 5 focused controllers under Controllers/Admin/.
phases.risk: Medium — URL routes must stay identical to avoid breaking the Angular frontend
notes: All new controllers keep [Route('api/admin/...')] to preserve existing API contracts.
The shared IsAdminAsync() check should be replaced by injecting ITokenService and calling it inline — or extract to a base class AdminControllerBase.

steps.description: Optional but recommended: create an abstract base class that injects ITokenService and UserManager and exposes a protected Task<bool> IsAdminAsync() so the 5 derived controllers don't repeat it.
content_outline: protected async Task<bool> IsAdminAsync() — reads Bearer token via _tokenService, checks IsInRoleAsync('Admin'), falls back to cookie claim

steps.description: Move GetAllUsers(), UpdatePlayerRating(), UpdateUserProfile() from AdminController. Route: [Route('api/admin')].
Move GetAllSessions(), GetSession(), CreateSession(), UpdateSession(), DeleteSession(), PublishDraft() from AdminController. Route: [Route('api/admin')].
Move GetSessionRegistrations(), AddManualRegistration(), UpdateRegistration(), RemoveRegistration(), GetAllRegistrations() from AdminController.
Move GetAllLeagues() and UpdateLeague() from AdminController.
Move GetDashboardAnalytics() from AdminController.
Delete the original AdminController.cs once all endpoints are verified to exist in the new controllers and all tests pass.

phases.name: Update Tests to Use Repository Mocks
phases.goal: Replace InMemoryDatabase test setup with proper interface mocks (Moq) so tests are true unit tests, not integration tests.
phases.risk: Low — test project only, no production code changes

steps.description: Add Moq package: dotnet add package Moq
The factory can remain for integration tests. No changes required for existing integration tests.
Example unit test: mock ISessionRepository, ISessionActivationService, ITokenService. Instantiate AdminSessionsController directly. No HTTP server, no database.
content_outline: var controller = new AdminSessionsController(mockRepo.Object, mockActivation.Object, mockToken.Object, mockUserManager, mockLogger);

steps.description: Unit test the new TokenService in isolation: test token generation, expiry, invalid base64, mismatched email, valid round-trip.

execution_order: Phase 1 — ITokenService (no dependencies, safe to do first, builds immediately)
Phase 2 — ISessionActivationService (no dependencies, safe to do in parallel with Phase 1)
Phase 5 — Repository Pattern (depends on Phase 4 for clean model namespaces)
Phase 6 — Split AdminController (depends on Phases 1–5 so the new controllers are clean from the start)
Phase 7 — Update Tests (depends on Phases 5 and 6)
testing_checkpoints: After Phase 1: run existing test suite — all tests must still pass
After Phase 2: manually verify session activation/deactivation still works via Swagger
After Phase 3: verify session registration payment flow end-to-end
After Phase 4: dotnet build must have zero errors
After Phase 5 step 5.10: run full test suite — integration tests still use InMemoryDatabase so they should pass
After Phase 6: run full test suite AND manually hit every api/admin/* endpoint via Swagger to verify routes are unchanged
After Phase 7: unit tests must pass without any database
